Fuji Media Holdings ( (JP:4676) ) has issued an update.

Fuji Media Holdings and its subsidiary, Fuji Television Network, have initiated significant reforms to address human rights and compliance issues, aiming to prevent future occurrences. The reforms include legal accountability for former executives, structural changes, and increased female representation in leadership roles. These measures are part of a broader strategy to enhance governance and restore stakeholder trust.

The most recent analyst rating on (JP:4676) stock is a Buy with a Yen2420.00 price target. To see the full list of analyst forecasts on Fuji Media Holdings stock, see the JP:4676 Stock Forecast page.

More about Fuji Media Holdings

Fuji Media Holdings, Inc. operates in the media and entertainment industry, primarily focusing on television broadcasting through its subsidiary, Fuji Television Network, Inc. The company is listed on the Tokyo Stock Exchange and is involved in various media-related activities, including content production and distribution.

Sichuan Expressway Co ( (HK:0107) ) just unveiled an update.

Sichuan Expressway Company Limited has announced a change in its principal place of business in Hong Kong to the 40th Floor, Dah Sing Financial Centre, effective from July 3, 2025. Additionally, Mr. Wong Wai Chiu has been appointed as the process agent to accept service of process and notices on behalf of the company in Hong Kong. These changes are part of the company’s strategic adjustments to enhance its operational efficiency and compliance with local regulations.

More about Sichuan Expressway Co

Sichuan Expressway Company Limited is a joint stock limited company incorporated in the People’s Republic of China, primarily engaged in the construction, operation, and management of expressways. The company focuses on providing transportation infrastructure services, which are crucial for regional connectivity and economic development.

Horseshoe Metals Limited ( (AU:HOR) ) just unveiled an update.

Horseshoe Metals Limited has issued 70,452,381 fully paid ordinary shares as part of its placement and debt-to-equity conversions. This issuance, not requiring shareholder approval, falls under the exemption provided by section 708A(5) of the Corporations Act 2001, allowing for the on-sale of these securities without disclosure. This move is aimed at strengthening the company’s financial position and potentially enhancing its market operations.

More about Horseshoe Metals Limited

Horseshoe Metals Limited operates in the mining industry, focusing on the exploration and development of copper and gold projects. The company is listed on the Australian Securities Exchange under the ticker ASX:HOR.
